title: Stallari Managed-Binary Capability Manifest
description: Runtime capability + lifecycle manifest for a binary supervised by stallari-supervisord. Companion to the build manifest at stallari-managed-binaries/manifests/<binary>.yaml. Consumed at spawn time to synthesise a sandbox-exec profile (DD-252 Phase C).

spec_version.description: Schema major.minor; the daemon refuses to spawn under a manifest whose major version it does not understand.

binary.description: Binary name. Must match the `binary:` field of the sibling build manifest at stallari-managed-binaries/manifests/<binary>.yaml.

purpose.description: Human-readable description of what this binary does and why Stallari supervises it. Surfaced in Settings → Add-ons.

keep_alive.description: Whether supervisord restarts the binary after exit.

restart_on_exit.description: Restart policy. `always` = restart on any exit. `unless_clean` = only restart on non-zero exit. `never` = no automatic restart.

graceful_shutdown_signal.description: Signal supervisord sends for graceful shutdown.

graceful_shutdown_timeout_seconds.description: Time to wait for the binary to exit cleanly after the graceful signal before sending SIGKILL.

working_dir.description: Process working directory. Either a StallariPaths.Class token (e.g. 'state(.managed("gatus"))') or an absolute path. Supervisord resolves tokens at spawn time.

config_path.description: Optional path supervisord writes daemon-managed config to. Binary reads via env var (declared in `communication`) or its own --config flag.

read_only_mounts.description: Additional read-only filesystem subtrees beyond working_dir. Each entry must be either a StallariPaths.Class token or an absolute path. Use sparingly — every mount widens the sandbox.

write_mounts.description: Additional writable filesystem subtrees beyond working_dir. Empty array (the default) means working_dir is the only writable location. Use only when the binary genuinely cannot write under working_dir.

egress.description: Default egress posture. `deny` = no outbound network. `allow_with_allowlist` = outbound only to entries in egress_allowlist (must be non-empty).

egress_allowlist.description: Allowed outbound hosts when egress is `allow_with_allowlist`. Each entry: `<host-pattern>:<port>`. Supports wildcard subdomain (e.g. `*.example.com:443`). When egress is `deny`, this MUST be empty.

ingress.description: Inbound listening ports. Empty = no listeners. 127.0.0.1 binds are loopback-only (daemon proxies external access). 0.0.0.0 binds require `privileged: true` (and a rationale field; rejected by the secops-scanner rule).

memory_max_mb.description: Max resident memory in megabytes. Enforced via setrlimit(RLIMIT_AS) under daemon-best-effort, and via Seatbelt under kernel enforcement.

cpu_quota_percent.description: Max CPU usage as a percentage of one core. Soft ceiling — used for runaway detection rather than hard enforcement.

privileged.description: Opt-in flag for binaries needing extra capabilities (raw sockets, 0.0.0.0 binds, etc.). v1.0 schema accepts the field for forward-compat but disallows `true` values until DD-252 Phase C Q4 nails the semantics. Setting `true` requires a sibling `rationale:` field (enforced by the secops-scanner rule, not the schema).

rationale.description: Free-form justification for `privileged: true` (when v1.1 enables it) or for unusual mount / network shapes. Surfaced in catalog review.

communication.description: How supervisord + daemon talk to the binary. At least one entry required.

pathExpression.description: Either a StallariPaths.Class token or an absolute filesystem path. Tokens have the form `<class>(<args>)` where <class> is one of state/cache/runtime/config/log and <args> follows Swift enum syntax.

oneOf.description: StallariPaths.Class token — resolved by supervisord at spawn time. Allows nested parens (e.g. `state(.managed("gatus"))`) and optional sub-path suffix.
Absolute POSIX path. Use sparingly; prefer StallariPaths tokens.

path_prefix.description: Optional URL path prefix supervisord proxies to (e.g. '/api'). Defaults to '/'.

ring_buffer_lines.description: Lines retained in supervisord's per-binary ring buffer (default 4096).

transport.description: Wire format. Reserved for future use; v1.0 accepts only 'stdio' to match DD-242's stdio-only blade-MCP transport invariant.
